CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["body"],"properties":{"body":{"type":"string","description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-01T20:34:33.3439713Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-01T20:34:14.798930247Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"57679","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-01T20:34:33.3461486Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-01T20:34:14.998948734Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57679,\"title\":\"[DataFactory] TestRecordingMismatchException with ProjRef=true caused by Storage TypeSpec migration request pattern change\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nDataFactory tests fail with `TestRecordingMismatchException` when run with `UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true`. Root cause is the Storage TypeSpec migration (https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896) which changed the HTTP request creation pattern, shifting the `Recording.Random` sequence and causing resource name mismatches during playback.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe AutoRest-generated Storage client created the LRO request object **twice** per operation — once to send and once for LRO tracking:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Old (AutoRest) — 2 Random.NewGuid() consumed per PUT\\nvar response = await _storageAccountRestClient.CreateAsync(...);\\nvar operation = new StorageArmOperation\\u0026lt;\\u0026gt;(...,\\n    _storageAccountRestClient.CreateCreateRequest(...).Request, // extra CreateRequest() call\\n    response, ...);\\n```\\n\\nThe TypeSpec-generated code correctly reuses the request, creating it only **once**:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// New (TypeSpec) — 1 Random.NewGuid() consumed per PUT\\nHttpMessage message = _storageAccountsRestClient.CreateCreateRequest(...);\\nResponse response = await Pipeline.ProcessMessageAsync(message, context);\\nvar operation = new StorageArmOperation\\u0026lt;\\u0026gt;(..., message.Request, response, ...);\\n```\\n\\nEach `CreateRequest()` call goes through `ProxyTransport.CreateRequest()` which consumes `Recording.Random.NewGuid()`. The eliminated redundant call shifts the entire `Random` sequence, causing all subsequent `Recording.GenerateAssetName()` calls to produce different suffixes — e.g., `adf-linkedservice-mws-9555` instead of the recorded `adf-linkedservice-mws-2175`.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n```powershell\\n# PASSES (NuGet packages)\\ndotnet test eng/service.proj --filter \\u0026#34;FullyQualifiedName~Dataset_AmazonMwsObject_Create\\u0026#34; `\\n  /p:ServiceDirectory=datafactory /p:UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=false\\n\\n# FAILS (source project references)\\ndotnet test eng/service.proj --filter \\u0026#34;FullyQualifiedName~Dataset_AmazonMwsObject_Create\\u0026#34; `\\n  /p:ServiceDirectory=datafactory /p:UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true\\n```\\n\\n## Why PR #56896 Passed CI\\n\\nThe Storage PR (https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896) pipeline only runs tests for `ServiceDirectory: storage`. It does not run downstream consumers (like DataFactory) that depend on Storage via `UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true`. DataFactory\\u0026#39;s `ci.mgmt.yml` has no path trigger for `sdk/storage/` changes.\\n\\n## Related PRs and Issues\\n\\n- **Root cause PR**: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896 — Storage TypeSpec migration that changed request creation pattern\\n- **Observed failure**: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57668 — Azure.Identity.Broker 1.5.0 release PR where DataFactory dependency tests failed\\n- **CI build with failure**: https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/29ec6040-b234-4e31-b139-33dc4287b756/_build/results?buildId=6094611\\n- **Prior occurrence**: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57478 — same DataFactory Dataset test failures observed\\n\\n## Key Files\\n\\n- **Failing tests**: `sdk/datafactory/Azure.ResourceManager.DataFactory/tests/Scenario/DataFactoryDatasetResourceTests.cs`\\n- **Test helper using Storage**: `sdk/datafactory/Azure.ResourceManager.DataFactory/tests/DataFactoryManagementTestBase.cs` (line 77-86, `GetStorageAccountAccessKey`)\\n- **Changed source (new pattern)**: `sdk/storage/Azure.ResourceManager.Storage/src/Generated/StorageAccountCollection.cs` (`CreateOrUpdateAsync`)\\n- **Random consumption**: `sdk/core/Azure.Core.TestFramework/src/ProxyTransport.cs` (line 143, `CreateRequest()` consumes `Random.NewGuid()`)\\n- **DataFactory CI config (missing path trigger)**: `sdk/datafactory/ci.mgmt.yml`\\n- **Test matrix with ProjRef**: `eng/pipelines/templates/stages/platform-matrix.json`\\n\\n## Recommended Fix\\n\\n1. **Short-term**: Re-record DataFactory tests (and any other downstream consumers of Storage) to align with the new request creation pattern.\\n2. **Long-term**: Add path triggers in downstream CI configs (e.g., DataFactory\\u0026#39;s `ci.mgmt.yml`) for dependency directories like `sdk/storage/`, so that dependency changes trigger downstream test matrices including `UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true`.\\n\\n## Affected Tests\\n\\nAll `Dataset_*` and `LinkedService_*` tests in `Azure.ResourceManager.DataFactory.Tests` that use `GetStorageAccountAccessKey()`, which calls `StorageAccounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync()` — the method whose request creation pattern changed in the TypeSpec migration.\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57679\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\",\"id\":64171366,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-nash\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-01T20:31:45Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-01T20:32:14Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Root cause is the Storage TypeSpec migration (https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896) which changed the HTTP request creation pattern, shifting the `Recording.Random` sequence and causing resource name mismatches during playback.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe AutoRest-generated Storage client created the LRO request object **twice** per operation — once to send and once for LRO tracking:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Old (AutoRest) — 2 Random.NewGuid() consumed per PUT\\nvar response = await _storageAccountRestClient.CreateAsync(...);\\nvar operation = new StorageArmOperation\\u0026lt;\\u0026gt;(...,\\n    _storageAccountRestClient.CreateCreateRequest(...).Request, // extra CreateRequest() call\\n    response, ...);\\n```\\n\\nThe TypeSpec-generated code correctly reuses the request, creating it only **once**:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// New (TypeSpec) — 1 Random.NewGuid() consumed per PUT\\nHttpMessage message = _storageAccountsRestClient.CreateCreateRequest(...);\\nResponse response = await Pipeline.ProcessMessageAsync(message, context);\\nvar operation = new StorageArmOperation\\u0026lt;\\u0026gt;(..., message.Request, response, ...);\\n```\\n\\nEach `CreateRequest()` call goes through `ProxyTransport.CreateRequest()` which consumes `Recording.Random.NewGuid()`. The eliminated redundant call shifts the entire `Random` sequence, causing all subsequent `Recording.GenerateAssetName()` calls to produce different suffixes — e.g., `adf-linkedservice-mws-9555` instead of the recorded `adf-linkedservice-mws-2175`.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n```powershell\\n# PASSES (NuGet packages)\\ndotnet test eng/service.proj --filter \\u0026#34;FullyQualifiedName~Dataset_AmazonMwsObject_Create\\u0026#34; `\\n  /p:ServiceDirectory=datafactory /p:UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=false\\n\\n# FAILS (source project references)\\ndotnet test eng/service.proj --filter \\u0026#34;FullyQualifiedName~Dataset_AmazonMwsObject_Create\\u0026#34; `\\n  /p:ServiceDirectory=datafactory /p:UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true\\n```\\n\\n## Why PR #56896 Passed CI\\n\\nThe Storage PR (https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896) pipeline only runs tests for `ServiceDirectory: storage`. It does not run downstream consumers (like DataFactory) that depend on Storage via `UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true`. DataFactory\\u0026#39;s `ci.mgmt.yml` has no path trigger for `sdk/storage/` changes.\\n\\n## Related PRs and Issues\\n\\n- **Root cause PR**: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56896 — Storage TypeSpec migration that changed request creation pattern\\n- **Observed failure**: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57668 — Azure.Identity.Broker 1.5.0 release PR where `sdk/storage/Azure.ResourceManager.Storage/src/Generated/StorageAccountCollection.cs` (`CreateOrUpdateAsync`)\\n- **Random consumption**: `sdk/core/Azure.Core.TestFramework/src/ProxyTransport.cs` (line 143, `CreateRequest()` consumes `Random.NewGuid()`)\\n- **DataFactory CI config (missing path trigger)**: `sdk/datafactory/ci.mgmt.yml`\\n- **Test matrix with ProjRef**: `eng/pipelines/templates/stages/platform-matrix.json`\\n\\n## Recommended Fix\\n\\n1. **Short-term**: Re-record DataFactory tests (and any other downstream consumers of Storage) to align with the new request creation pattern.\\n2. **Long-term**: Add path triggers in downstream CI configs (e.g., DataFactory\\u0026#39;s `ci.mgmt.yml`) for dependency directories like `sdk/storage/`, so that dependency changes trigger downstream test matrices including `UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true`.\\n\\n## Affected Tests\\n\\nAll `Dataset_*` and `LinkedService_*` tests in `Azure.ResourceManager.DataFactory.Tests` that use `GetStorageAccountAccessKey()`, which calls `StorageAccounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync()` — the method whose request creation pattern changed in the
